purpose: Claude Code skill definition for `p-track-resume`. It describes trigger conditions, workflow, constraints, and referenced protocol files for that skill. Key content: --- name: p-track-resume description: > Resume a stalled, interrupted, or partially completed Synapse swarm. Reconstructs state from dashboard files, checks agent health, re-dispatches stuck/failed tasks, and runs the full execution-to-completion lifecycle with comprehensive reporting. argument-hint: "[--dashboard <id>]" user-invocable: true context: fork model: opus --- The file is 381 lines long and should be treated as a knowledge/control-plane source rather than application code.
patterns: Skill-definition pattern: trigger guidance first, then workflow steps and referenced protocol files; these files are loaded on demand by Claude Code.
Uses explicit NON-NEGOTIABLE language to encode hard behavioral constraints for agents.
Uses fenced code blocks for command examples, schemas, or prompt templates.
Uses markdown tables for compact command, file, or option matrices.
conventions: Markdown headings use a single H1 followed by H2/H3 sections for navigable documentation.
Agent-control documents use `{tracker_root}`, `{project_root}`, and `{dashboardId}` placeholders instead of machine-specific paths.
ASCII double hyphen is used in prose where many docs avoid Unicode punctuation for terminal readability.
